ls_sharpe_decomposition.method: DIAGNOSTIC over the already-SPENT OOS (no seal claimed, no fresh-OOS pass). run_batch_screening(engine=batch, n_quantiles=5, horizons=5/10/20) over the factor recomputed via compute_factors(stage=oos_test); full-window reproduces the registered 7.24/2.59 + 0.131/0.070 EXACTLY (machinery validated). why_so_different_vs_prior.no_methodology_discrepancy: full-window reproduces the 2026-06-09 approval EXACTLY (0.131/7.24, 0.070/2.59). Same code/path. The 'difference' is a WINDOW SPLIT of the same single OOS number, not a re-evaluation.
why_so_different_vs_prior.concentration: ALL headline strength is in the 16-month deep window (2021-01..2022-04): ICIR_20d 0.364/0.314, LS 8.65/3.58. The 44-month clean window is 0.041/neg. Full = the blend.
why_so_different_vs_prior.the_tell: the deep-window OOS ICIR (0.36/0.31) is at IN-SAMPLE level (~13%/8% decay from IS 0.42/0.34). Genuine OOS decays ~70-90% (the clean window does). An 'OOS' sub-window performing at IS level, on EXACTLY the JQ-consensus-backfilled/approximated-breadth period, is the signature of a data-RECONSTRUCTION artifact (the 2022-05 backfill carries information not available in real time), NOT a favorable regime (regime does not lift OOS IC to IS levels).
why_so_different_vs_prior.regime_confound: 2022-05 (data-trust boundary) ≈ the 2022 growth/momentum crash (regime boundary) — confounded. But magnitude (IS-level OOS on the backfilled portion) favors the artifact reading.
why_so_different_vs_prior.cannot_fully_separate: definitive separation needs a truly point-in-time per-revision deep-history breadth, which is UNRECOVERABLE (Tushare granular-not-PIT / JQ PIT-not-granular scissors). The trustworthy per-row window (2022-05+) is the only honest OOS, and there the factor is weak (_60) / dead (_120).

oos_decay_pit_attribution.question: is the OOS decay because the analyst factor STILL has a PIT problem?
oos_decay_pit_attribution.structural: create_time real per-row only 2022-05+; IS(2014-2020) + deep-OOS(2021-01..2022-04) breadth are BOTH the frozen 2022-05 JQ backfill. So the IS 0.42 benchmark was NEVER PIT-clean — same compromised vintage as the deep OOS. The ONLY real-per-row evaluation is the clean 2022-05+ window.

lag_stress.deep_backfill_lag_invariant: _60 ICIR 0.364@+0td -> 0.371@+5td; _120 0.314 -> 0.317. Pushing the visibility anchor 5td LATER does NOT erode the IC -> the 'signal' is baked into the reconstructed VALUES irrespective of read timing -> a DATA-VINTAGE PIT artifact (the 2022-05 JQ reconstruction encodes end-state consensus retroactively, not as-of). No anchor/lag fixes it because the values themselves are not point-in-time. Lag-invariance also RULES OUT a favorable-regime explanation (a real signal decays with staleness; this does not).
lag_stress.clean_lag_decays: _60 ICIR 0.041@+0 -> 0.033@+2 -> 0.020@+5 -> 0.005@+10td (steep decay toward 0); _120 already negative. Pushing the anchor LATER removes the signal -> the (weak) clean signal is REAL and TIMELY (alpha in the fresh revision, decaying with staleness) — the OPPOSITE of a lookahead (a lookahead inflates early reads and vanishes when pushed to the correct time). So the clean window is PIT-CLEAN; the deployed create_time+1 anchor is the honest read.

oos_decay_pit_attribution.answer: TWO layers. (1) The backfilled deep history (IS + deep OOS) DOES carry a PIT problem — a data-VINTAGE artifact (lag-invariant => values not as-of), which is the root cause of the inflated 0.42/0.36. The user's instinct is correct for the STRONG numbers. (2) The clean 2022-05+ window does NOT have a residual PIT problem (signal decays with staleness => genuine + timely); the deployed anchor is honest. The factor is simply genuinely WEAK there (_60 0.041) / DEAD (_120 negative). So the decay is: backfill-vintage PIT inflation removed -> the factor's true PIT-clean weak edge revealed. It is NOT a live-anchoring PIT bug.
oos_decay_pit_attribution.caveat: the factor has NO clean evidence of ever being strong: every strong number is on the unrecoverable 2022-05 backfill vintage; its only PIT-clean evaluation is weak/dead.

verdict_INITIAL_SUPERSEDED.note: INITIAL Tushare-only verdict (2026-06-15 AM) — SUPERSEDED by the JoinQuant genuine-PIT benchmark below. Preserved for the audit trail.
verdict_INITIAL_SUPERSEDED.correction_reason: I argued the pre-2022 Tushare strength was a backfill VINTAGE artifact based on the lag-invariance of the deep window (ICIR 0.36 -> 0.37 at +5td). That inference was STRUCTURALLY WRONG for a slow-moving consensus signal: a 60-120d rolling breadth on slow analyst-consensus drift is INHERENTLY lag-insensitive at small +td shifts WHETHER the values are first-state or end-state. Lag-invariance was equally consistent with 'real persistent signal' and 'vintage artifact' -> the test could not discriminate.

joinquant_pit_benchmark_2026_06_15.directive: user: replicate IS evaluation on JoinQuant 朝阳永续 (genuinely-PIT) as the artifact-vs-decay discriminator
joinquant_pit_benchmark_2026_06_15.method: JoinQuant cloud research: monthly as-of grid (survivorship-correct universes via get_all_securities(date=asof)), VALIDATED PIT consensus EP series predicted_earnings_to_price_ratio (the 朝阳永续 PIT level), reconstruct genuine-PIT consensus FY1 EPS = EP * RAW close (price cancels exactly -> earnings), take 2m/4m EPS-revision as the breadth proxy, score RankIC vs 5/10/20d adj fwd returns. Two windows: PRE 2014-2021 (n=87 month-ends), POST 2022-05+ (n=44).
joinquant_pit_benchmark_2026_06_15.two_pit_sources_two_signal_constructions: Tushare per-analyst breadth vs JQ consensus-EPS revision — DIFFERENT signal constructions, INDEPENDENT data sources, agree on ICIR magnitude in BOTH windows (~0.30 PRE / ~0.04 POST). The probability of matching by coincidence is ~zero -> the pre-2022 strength is the REAL underlying analyst-sentiment-shift economic signal, and the post-2022 weakness is REAL decay.
joinquant_pit_benchmark_2026_06_15.scissors_resolved: the 'Tushare granular-not-PIT / JQ PIT-not-granular' scissors does NOT actually block this question, because both proxies measure the same economic signal (analyst sentiment shift) and the IC magnitudes match across both windows.

gpt_5_5_pro_cross_review_verdict_2026_06.procedural_status_now: BOTH factors stay candidate; attach diagnostic memo + decay context; do NOT mutate the registry
gpt_5_5_pro_cross_review_verdict_2026_06.the_load_bearing_finding_Q7: I conflated 'the underlying signal is real' with 'the canary contingency is discharged'. They are different. The 2026-06-09 approval was explicitly CANARY-OVERRIDDEN: the user accepted the risk that the verdict would be meaningless AND the unburned OOS permanently lost if contamination later appeared. The 2026-06-14 canary then LITERALLY FIRED. The revoke followed the contingency rule. Reversing the revoke requires NOT 'metrics are real' but 'canary contingency should not have triggered' or 'is explicitly overridden again under a NEW audit record'. GPT: 'the default precedent should be: the revoke stands unless the canary is formally discharged as non-material or explicitly overridden again.'
gpt_5_5_pro_cross_review_verdict_2026_06.Q1_jq_pit_validity: OK as independent DIAGNOSTIC; not sufficient by itself for re-approval. The JQ PIT-validation artifact (level corr +0.997 / Spearman +0.94 vs Tushare) must be archived alongside as load-bearing committed evidence.
gpt_5_5_pro_cross_review_verdict_2026_06.Q2_eps_reconstruction: CHANGES REQUIRED — magnitude match doesn't rule out a price-basis mismatch. Need a price-basis sensitivity table (EP * raw, EP * pre-adj, EP * post-adj, EP-revision alone, direct JQ consensus EPS if available) with per-month rank_corr + decile overlap + ICIR by variant.
gpt_5_5_pro_cross_review_verdict_2026_06.Q3_consensus_vs_breadth_proxy: CHANGES REQUIRED for re-approval; OK for diagnosis. The cross-source IC magnitude match supports 'historical analyst-revision regime', but does NOT prove the EXACT Tushare per-analyst breadth factor was PIT-clean (per-analyst breadth can diverge from consensus-level revision in: many small revisions vs one large; coverage changes; broker stale forecasts; earnings-level revision without breadth; breadth change with tiny EPS magnitude). Required: a direct JQ PIT analyst-up/down-count factor OR a bridge test on the overlapping clean window — Tushare breadth vs JQ consensus revision monthly cross-sectional rank correlation + decile overlap + IC contribution after residualization.
gpt_5_5_pro_cross_review_verdict_2026_06.Q4_pre_registered_honesty: CHANGES REQUIRED — observed mean_rank_ic 0.024 is BELOW the stated [0.03, 0.06] band; ICIR 0.339 is squarely in [0.3, 0.4]. Pre-registered status: MARGINAL pass (passed ICIR criterion partly for _60 and _120; mean-IC criterion below stated lower band). Not a slam dunk. The honest framing: 'JQ supports artifact-vs-decay diagnosis, but not automatic status restoration.' Also: GPT got a 404 trying to fetch the CSV at the brief's path during review — verify the committed location resolves (it should: data/external/jq_eps_diffusion_benchmark.csv is committed in b5d70a0).
gpt_5_5_pro_cross_review_verdict_2026_06.Q5_lag_invariance_reversal_overcorrectio: CHANGES REQUIRED — I overcorrected. The lesson 'lag-stress is NOT a vintage discriminator for slow-moving consensus signals' is TOO BROAD. Correct statement: 'Short-lag invariance on a slow rolling consensus signal is NON-DECISIVE. It neither proves nor rules out a vintage artifact.' A lag test CAN still diagnose artifacts if designed properly (+20/+40/+60td on a 60d window; endpoint-displacement attribution; event-time decay by revision date; first-known vs final-state snapshot if available). Both my old inference (+5d invariance proves artifact) AND my new inference (lag can't discriminate) were too strong — the truth is in the middle.
gpt_5_5_pro_cross_review_verdict_2026_06.Q6_post_2022_decay_overstated: CHANGES REQUIRED — the POST window evidence supports 'no observed post-2022 edge' but NOT 'statistically proven decay to zero'. With n=44 month-ends the POST _60 result is statistically indistinguishable from zero. Correct framing: 'post-2022 PIT evidence shows no reliable current edge across two PIT sources; decay/regime-change is the leading interpretation, but the window is LOW-POWER and the claim is not statistically established.'
gpt_5_5_pro_cross_review_verdict_2026_06.Q8_deployment_caveat: OK, and MANDATORY if any re-approval override ever happens — must explicitly include in the approval reason: 'historical full-window OOS positive but dominated by 2021-2022; post-2022 PIT-clean evidence shows no reliable current standalone edge; deployment gate remains failed / not deployable.' The 'approved factor != deployable strategy' standing convention is too easy to misread because the headline LS 7.24 is visually strong.

paths_to_eventual_reversal_per_gpt.summary: Recompute the EXACT eps_diffusion_60/_120 factor panels under SNAP1 (2026-06-07) vs SNAP2 (2026-06-14) data and show FACTOR-LEVEL immateriality (not just EPS-payload immateriality already shown): rank_corr > 0.999, decile membership drift below tolerance, IC/LS metrics unchanged within tolerance, affected rows explain zero material signal change. If clean -> canary formally discharged as non-material -> revoke procedurally reversible. THIS IS THE RIGOROUS PATH.

Explicitly document that the original 'zero backfilled + zero drift' canary bar was MIS-SPECIFIED, replace with a materiality bar, sign a SECOND override that says 'this is a governance override of the canary contingency, not a new OOS pass'. Less clean but valid.
Approve only after enough post-2026 fresh labels accumulate under a NEW FrozenSelectionSet. The spent c5335681... set cannot become fresh again.

oos_status: 2021-2026 OOS remains SPENT (FrozenSelectionSet c5335681...). All re-evaluation since the revoke is post-hoc diagnostic on already-observed data, NOT new spends. The OOS cannot be treated as fresh again under any path.
lessons_after_GPT_review: Short-lag invariance on a slow rolling consensus signal is NON-DECISIVE — neither proves nor rules out a vintage artifact. Both my +5d-invariance-proves-artifact (initial) and lag-can't-discriminate (corrected) framings were too strong.

Two-PIT-sources-agreeing on IC magnitudes IS strong evidence for a real underlying economic signal, BUT it does NOT prove the EXACT factor was PIT-clean (proxies can converge on aggregate IC while diverging at the per-stock signal level).
Substantive evidence (signal real) does NOT discharge procedural contingency (canary fired) — these are independent and BOTH must clear for a status reversal.

When approving with a CANARY-OVERRIDE, the operating principle must be: if the canary later fires, the revoke stands by default and reversal needs FACTOR-LEVEL discharge or explicit second governance override, not a re-attribution of the underlying signal.
When I'm self-correcting my own prior verdict, the bias toward over-correction is real — independent cross-review (GPT here) is load-bearing to keep me honest.

Pre-registered interpretation MATTERS, AND should be precise (the brief said 'mean_rank_ic 0.03-0.06+ ICIR 0.3-0.4'; observed mean_rank_ic 0.024 was BELOW the band — that's a marginal pass not a slam dunk, and the response doc must say so).
